title: Ectopic Pregnancy
objectives: Identify risk factors for ectopic pregnancy
Recognize ruptured ectopic as surgical emergency

vignette.text:
A 29-year-old woman presents with 6 weeks of amenorrhea, lower abdominal pain, and vaginal spotting for 2 days. Her last menstrual period was 6 weeks ago. She has a history of chlamydia infection treated 2 years ago.

Past Medical History: Chlamydia (treated), no prior pregnancies

Medications: None

Social History: Sexually active with one male partner, inconsistent contraception use

physicalExam.abdomen: Soft, mild left lower quadrant tenderness, no rebound or guarding
physicalExam.pelvic: Closed cervical os, mild left adnexal tenderness, no masses palpable

imaging.Transvaginal Ultrasound: No intrauterine pregnancy. Small amount of free fluid in pelvis. 2.5 cm complex left adnexal mass.

questions.question: At what beta-hCG level should an intrauterine pregnancy be visible on transvaginal ultrasound?
questions.explanation: The discriminatory zone is 1,500-2,000 mIU/mL for TVUS (3,000-4,000 for transabdominal). Above this level, a normal IUP should be visible. Absence of IUP above the discriminatory zone strongly suggests ectopic.

questions.question: Which patient is a candidate for methotrexate treatment of ectopic pregnancy?
questions.explanation: MTX candidates: hemodynamically stable, unruptured, no fetal cardiac activity, mass <3.5cm, beta-hCG ideally <5000 (some use higher cutoffs), able to follow up, no contraindications (liver/kidney disease, immunodeficiency).

keyTakeaways: Risk factors: prior ectopic, PID, tubal surgery, IUD, infertility treatment
Discriminatory zone: 1,500-2,000 mIU/mL - IUP should be visible above this
Triad: amenorrhea + abdominal pain + vaginal bleeding
Methotrexate for stable, unruptured ectopic meeting criteria

mcqs.stem: Which of the following is the MOST important initial step in evaluating a patient with suspected ectopic pregnancy?

options.explanation: Treatment should generally follow an appropriate diagnostic evaluation unless the patient is critically unstable.

options.text: Obtain a thorough history and physical examination
options.explanation: A comprehensive history and physical examination is the foundation of clinical reasoning in ectopic pregnancy. It guides all subsequent diagnostic and management decisions.

options.text: Consult a specialist before any workup
options.explanation: Initial evaluation should be performed by the treating physician; specialist consultation follows based on findings.
Advanced imaging may be indicated but should be guided by clinical findings, not ordered reflexively.

correct.reinforcement: Correct! Obtain a thorough history and physical examination.
correct.deeperWhy: Understanding this concept is foundational for clinical reasoning in this domain.
correct.clinicalPearl: Remember: Obtain a thorough history and physical examination

coachDx.misconception: Initial evaluation should be performed by the treating physician; specialist consultation follows based on findings.
coachDx.guidance: Review the key features that distinguish Obtain a thorough history and physical examination from Consult a specialist before any workup.
coachDx.bridgeTo: The key distinction involves Obtain a thorough history and physical examination.

mcqs.stem: A patient presents with classic features of ectopic pregnancy. Which clinical reasoning principle is MOST important in avoiding diagnostic error?

options.explanation: Systematic consideration of differential diagnoses, including must-not-miss conditions, is essential for avoiding premature closure and anchoring bias.

options.text: Search satisficing - stop looking after the first diagnosis
options.explanation: Premature closure (stopping the diagnostic process too early) is a common cognitive error that leads to missed diagnoses.
While pattern recognition (System 1 thinking) is useful, it should be balanced with analytical reasoning (System 2) to avoid cognitive biases.

options.text: Order every available test to rule out all possibilities
options.explanation: Indiscriminate testing leads to false positives, unnecessary procedures, and increased cost without improving diagnostic accuracy.

correct.reinforcement: Correct! Consider the differential diagnosis systematically.
correct.deeperWhy: Understanding this concept is foundational for clinical reasoning in this domain.
correct.clinicalPearl: Remember: Consider the differential diagnosis systematically

coachDx.misconception: Indiscriminate testing leads to false positives, unnecessary procedures, and increased cost without improving diagnostic accuracy.
coachDx.guidance: Review the key features that distinguish Consider the differential diagnosis systematically from Order every available test to rule out all possibilities.
coachDx.bridgeTo: The key distinction involves Consider the differential diagnosis systematically.

mcqs.stem: When managing ectopic pregnancy, which approach best supports evidence-based practice?

options.text: Integrate current best evidence with clinical expertise and patient values
options.explanation: Evidence-based medicine is the conscientious integration of best research evidence, clinical expertise, and patient values in making clinical decisions.

options.text: Always defer to the most recent published study
options.explanation: Individual studies should be appraised for quality and relevance; systematic reviews and guidelines generally provide stronger evidence.

options.text: Follow institutional protocols regardless of patient factors
options.explanation: Evidence-based practice integrates best evidence with clinical expertise AND patient values/circumstances.

options.text: Base decisions solely on clinical experience
options.explanation: Clinical experience is valuable but can introduce bias; it should be combined with current evidence.

correct.reinforcement: Correct! Integrate current best evidence with clinical expertise and patient values.
correct.deeperWhy: Understanding this concept is foundational for clinical reasoning in this domain.
correct.clinicalPearl: Remember: Integrate current best evidence with clinical expertise and patient values

coachDx.misconception: Individual studies should be appraised for quality and relevance; systematic reviews and guidelines generally provide stronger evidence.
coachDx.guidance: Review the key features that distinguish Integrate current best evidence with clinical expertise and patient values from Always defer to the most recent published study.
coachDx.bridgeTo: The key distinction involves Integrate current best evidence with clinical expertise and patient values.

mcqs.stem: A G2P1 at 38 weeks presents with painless bright red vaginal bleeding. Ultrasound shows a complete placenta previa. What is the recommended management?

options.text: Attempt vaginal delivery with close monitoring
options.explanation: Vaginal delivery is absolutely contraindicated with complete placenta previa — the placenta completely covers the cervical os, and cervical dilation would cause catastrophic hemorrhage.

options.text: Digital cervical exam to assess dilation
options.explanation: Digital cervical exam is CONTRAINDICATED in placenta previa — it can disrupt the placenta and precipitate massive hemorrhage. Always confirm placental location by ultrasound before any cervical examination in third-trimester bleeding.

options.text: Expectant management with bed rest until 40 weeks
options.explanation: At 38 weeks with active bleeding and complete previa, expectant management risks maternal hemorrhage. Delivery is indicated.

options.text: Scheduled cesarean delivery at 36-37 weeks if stable; emergent C-section if hemorrhaging
options.explanation: Per ACOG, complete placenta previa is an absolute contraindication to vaginal delivery due to risk of life-threatening hemorrhage. If asymptomatic, planned cesarean at 36-37+6 weeks is recommended. Active hemorrhage at any gestational age requires emergent cesarean. At 38 weeks with bleeding, proceed to delivery.

correct.reinforcement: Correct! Scheduled cesarean delivery at 36-37 weeks if stable; emergent C-section if hemorrhaging.
correct.deeperWhy: Understanding this concept is foundational for clinical reasoning in this domain.
correct.clinicalPearl: Remember: Scheduled cesarean delivery at 36-37 weeks if stable; emergent C-section if hemorrhaging

coachDx.misconception: Digital cervical exam is CONTRAINDICATED in placenta previa — it can disrupt the placenta and precipitate massive hemorrhage. Always confirm placental location by ultrasound before any cervical examin
coachDx.guidance: Review the key features that distinguish Scheduled cesarean delivery at 36-37 weeks if stable; emergent C-section if hemo from Digital cervical exam to assess dilation.
coachDx.bridgeTo: The key distinction involves Scheduled cesarean delivery at 36-37 weeks if stable; emergent C-section if hemorrhaging.
